claims: The skill requests excessive actions beyond its core purpose of creating GitHub releases: 1) It requires full repository access including `git fetch --all` which fetches all remote branches and tags, exceeding the minimal needs for checking recent commits. 2) It automatically saves screenshots to `~/Downloads` without user consent, capturing potentially sensitive browser content (including the user's GitHub account interface) unrelated to release creation. 3) The skill includes hardcoded references to a specific repository (`Coooolfan/XiaomiAlbumSyncer`), suggesting it may not be general-purpose and could perform actions on unintended repositories. While local git access is necessary for generating release notes, the combination of these actions represents overreach for a release creation tool.
